With top leadership actively opposed and only ~6 weeks until July 1, restoration would require an extremely unlikely policy reversal. 5% represents the minimum probability given inherent uncertainty in political systems.

After comprehensive searching, I found no evidence of any imminent presidential action on Diversity Visa before July 1, 2025. The most direct evidence against this comes from a Law Stack Exchange post dated April 4, 2025 (search_1_summary), which states the Trump 2.0 administration 'has not yet, at least, sought to disrupt' the Diversity Visa program. This is relatively recent (only ~6 weeks before the knowledge cutoff) and directly addresses the question. Multiple searches for whitehouse.gov actions, May 2025 interviews, and recent statements found no mentions of Diversity Visa. There's plenty of historical opposition from Trump and policy proposals (Project 2025, Rep. Collins bill), but these haven't translated into concrete executive action plans. With only 42 days between the knowledge cutoff (May 20) and the deadline (July 1), and no recent signals or mentions, this makes the probability quite low. The administration appears focused on deportations and other visa categories rather than Diversity Visa specifically.

Based on comprehensive evidence: (1) Congressional action required to exclude foods from SNAP per legal expert (search_0_result_7), (2) statutory language permits "any food" with few exclusions, making website listings informational not legally binding (search_4_summary), (3) May 6 USDA press release focused on records/transparency with no eligibility changes mentioned (search_3_summary), (4) No FNS policy memos found about website modifications before May 20 cutoff, (5) Arkansas/Indiana waiver requests from April 15 show states pursuing restrictions, but waiver approval process typically takes months, (6) Nebraska waiver mentioned in May 20 article (search_5_result_2) but this is same day as cutoff - uncertain if approved. Combined with 6-week timeline from cutoff to July 1 resolution, extremely unlikely USDA would modify website without announced policy change. Probability 0.25 reflects small chance of surprise administrative action or technical website glitch.
